title: Building collapse: immediate movement and safe exit decision
summary: A rapid decision card for people inside or beside a newly damaged building: protect the head and airway, avoid falling hazards, leave only by a safer route, and never re-enter for property.
content:
A rapid decision card for people inside or beside a newly damaged building: protect the head and airway, avoid falling hazards, leave only by a safer route, and never re-enter for property.

Use the materials as a compact field kit. Work through the steps in order, pause whenever a warning or stop condition applies, and write down the outcome so another person can continue safely.
steps: Protect your head and neck, move away from glass, shelves, façades, and visibly sagging structural parts.
Check the nearest route without using lifts; if smoke, fire, exposed wiring, gas odour, or falling debris blocks it, choose a safer shelter point and call 112.
When a usable route exists, leave calmly, help only without entering unstable zones, and move to an open assembly area away from walls and power lines.
Account for people, report the exact address and observed hazards, and prevent anyone from re-entering until authorities declare it safe.
warnings: Aftershocks and secondary collapse can occur without warning.
Do not touch switches or create sparks when gas is suspected.
common_mistakes: Starting Building collapse: immediate movement and safe exit decision without first checking immediate danger and official instructions.
Failing to record time, location, responsible person, and unresolved risks for Building collapse: immediate movement and safe exit decision.
alternatives: Use a paper-only version of Building collapse: immediate movement and safe exit decision when power or connectivity is unavailable.
Assign one trained helper to simplify Building collapse: immediate movement and safe exit decision when the group is stressed or short-handed.
failure_signs: Conditions become more dangerous while carrying out Building collapse: immediate movement and safe exit decision.
Critical information cannot be confirmed or the assigned helper cannot continue Building collapse: immediate movement and safe exit decision safely.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue Building collapse: immediate movement and safe exit decision when it requires entering an unstable, contaminated, flooded, burning, electrically hazardous, or officially closed area.
Do not improvise medical, electrical, structural, or rescue actions beyond your training while using Building collapse: immediate movement and safe exit decision.
short_term: ['Within the first operational period, Building collapse: immediate movement and safe exit decision should produce a dated status, one responsible person, and a safe next action.', 'Repeat the essential check for Building collapse: immediate movement and safe exit decision whenever conditions, official advice, or available resources change.']
long_term: ['Include lessons from Building collapse: immediate movement and safe exit decision in the household or community emergency plan and resupply list.', 'Review the records from Building collapse: immediate movement and safe exit decision after the event and correct gaps before the next emergency.']
if_method_fails: ['If Building collapse: immediate movement and safe exit decision cannot be completed, move to the safest verified location, notify the responsible contact, and record what remains unresolved.', 'Use official emergency services or qualified specialists when the limits of Building collapse: immediate movement and safe exit decision are reached.']
environment_notes: ['Adapt Building collapse: immediate movement and safe exit decision to local terrain, weather, building type, transport access, language, disability needs, and official restrictions.', 'In Greece, use 112 for urgent emergencies and follow Civil Protection or local-authority instructions relevant to Building collapse: immediate movement and safe exit decision.']
